executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: Architecture-owned verifier/runtime topology is currently coherent; the remaining live red owner loop is marketing because Codeberg-primary adoption is still flat.
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: Let the queued marketing re-entry job fire at 2026-05-25T07:20:16.000Z and only recertify fully once marketing independent verification passes.

findings.title: Marketing remains the only live red owner loop
findings.mechanism: marketing independent verification is still fail and the latest workflow audit still shows flat Codeberg and GitHub adoption deltas across the recent window.
findings.root_cause: Outcome evidence is still missing on the Codeberg-primary conversion lane.
findings.recommended_fix: Use the queued marketing re-entry at 2026-05-25T07:20:16.000Z to produce a new measurable lane, then rerun marketing independent verification.

findings.title: Architecture audit metadata matches the live Gateway topology
findings.mechanism: Live runtime now shows 21 jobs, 21 enabled, 0 disabled, and the refreshed audit matches that state.
findings.root_cause: Direct live inspection and audit artifact are aligned in this run.
findings.recommended_fix: Preserve direct live-topology checks on every watchdog run; do not infer live-disabled jobs from persisted history.

findings.title: Architecture verifier path reran cleanly against refreshed live evidence
findings.mechanism: checker, independent verification, and verifier all pass for the architecture-owned path after the refreshed audit is in place.
findings.root_cause: Current architecture artifacts and live runtime evidence are coherent.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep rerunning independent verification after any material architecture refresh.

findings.title: Docs remains independently green and stable
findings.mechanism: Docs verifier is presently pass and no docs blocker is contributing a live architecture fault.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Use the remaining marketing re-entry job to generate fresh outcome evidence
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Marketing is still the only red owner domain after the architecture-side runtime checks came back clean.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Either clears the remaining blocker or sharpens the marketing repair target.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Rerun marketing independent verification after that execution
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: Current certification is still blocked by outcome evidence, not by architecture health.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Allows architecture to move from high_risk/qualified_pass to healthy once marketing passes.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Keep direct live-topology verification in the architecture watchdog
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: This path previously drifted when persisted history and live jobs were conflated.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: Prevents stale scheduler interpretation from producing false red architecture findings.

repairs_applied_this_run.change: Rebased the audit on the current 21-job live Gateway topology and current owner-loop artifacts.
Reran independent verification against the refreshed architecture audit and live runtime evidence.
Reran the architecture verifier after the fresh independent verification; the architecture-owned path is green.

independent_verification.summary: Independent verification confirms the architecture-owned verifier/runtime path is green and that the remaining blocker is external marketing outcome evidence.

what_still_needs_independent_verificatio: Fresh marketing independent pass after the re-entry job scheduled for 2026-05-25T07:20:16.000Z.

highest_risk_unresolved_issue.title: Marketing remains red on Codeberg-primary outcome evidence
highest_risk_unresolved_issue.why: Architecture-owned runtime checks are clean, but Codeberg adoption is still flat and marketing independent verification still fails closed.

notes: Architecture green here means architecture-owned localization is healthy; it does not mean marketing outcome evidence is green.
Persisted disabled jobs remain history only; live disabled jobs are zero.
The queued marketing-measurement-hold-release job is a valid future one-shot, not stale scheduler residue.
The remaining blocker is marketing outcome evidence, not an architecture self-certification fault.
Live snapshot in this refresh saw 6 running jobs and zero live-disabled jobs.
